key_insight: Large-scale machine-learned simulations reveal that amorphous indium oxide contains edge-sharing polyhedral chains and peroxide-like O-O bonds that explain its high mobility and intrinsic n-type behavior.
engineering_relevance: These structural fingerprints provide actionable guidance for designing amorphous oxide semiconductors and improving photoelectrochemical performance through controlled amorphization.
method: Trained a MACE machine-learned interatomic potential on first-principles molecular dynamics data.
Generated and analyzed amorphous structures with up to 5120 atoms, far beyond typical ab initio cell sizes.
Compared computed X-ray structure factors and vibrational signatures with experiment.
evidence_signal: The simulations reproduce experimental X-ray structure factors quantitatively and predict a Raman-active feature near 850 cm^-1 associated with peroxide-like bonds.
watch_item: The peroxide-like bond interpretation and quasi-crystalline regions should be validated across synthesis conditions and experimental probes, since amorphous networks can be sample-dependent.
paper_title: Large-scale first-principle simulations of amorphous indium oxide
